claim_text: The proposed model represents pedestrian travel time distribution more accurately than the constant speed model

The difference between necessary speed to complete crossing and current speed (Vnec - Vcur), conflict area dummy, and pedestrian flashing green (PFG) phase have significant impacts on acceleration/deceleration choices
The pedestrian speed change model provides significantly higher vehicle yield rates compared to constant speed assumptions, demonstrating its importance for safety assessment
Different models are needed for periods before and after PFG onset due to significantly different speed change behaviors
